note: Simplified reference summaries. Applies to one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses not more than 3 stories. For full code text, consult ICC at https://codes.iccsafe.org/

R101.title: Scope and Administration
R101.summary: The IRC applies to one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses not more than three stories above grade plane.

subsections.R101.2: Scope - Detached one- and two-family dwellings, townhouses not more than 3 stories

R301.title: Design Criteria
R301.summary: General design criteria for residential construction including wind, seismic, snow loads.

subsections.R301.1: Application - Buildings shall be constructed per design criteria
subsections.R301.2: Climatic and geographic design criteria - Wind speed, seismic design category, frost depth, termite, weathering
subsections.R301.5: Live load requirements (40 psf habitable, 30 psf sleeping, 10 psf attic without storage)

R302.title: Fire-Resistant Construction
R302.summary: Fire separation requirements between dwelling units, garages, and lot lines.

subsections.R302.1: Exterior walls - Fire-resistance ratings based on lot line distance (1-hr if <3ft, 0-hr if ≥5ft)
subsections.R302.2: Townhouse separation - 1-hour fire-resistance-rated wall or 2-hour common wall
subsections.R302.5: Dwelling/garage separation - 1/2 inch gypsum board minimum on garage side
subsections.R302.6: Dwelling/garage opening protection - Self-closing, tight-fitting door (not into sleeping room)

R303.title: Light, Ventilation, and Heating
R303.summary: Minimum natural light and ventilation requirements for habitable rooms.

subsections.R303.1: Habitable rooms - Min 8% of floor area glazing for natural light
subsections.R303.2: Adjoining rooms - Rooms may borrow light/ventilation from adjoining rooms
subsections.R303.3: Bathrooms - Mechanical ventilation (50 cfm intermittent or 20 cfm continuous) or openable window
subsections.R303.4: Mechanical ventilation - Outdoor air per IRC M1505
subsections.R303.7: Required heating - Must maintain 68°F at 3 ft above floor

R304.title: Minimum Room Areas
R304.summary: Minimum floor area and dimension requirements for habitable rooms.

subsections.R304.1: Minimum area - Every dwelling unit shall have at least one habitable room of not less than 120 sq ft
subsections.R304.2: Other rooms - Min 70 sq ft, min dimension 7 ft (except kitchens)
subsections.R304.3: Minimum dimensions - Not less than 7 ft in any horizontal dimension (except kitchens)
subsections.R304.4: Height effect on room area - Portions of room with ceiling height <5 ft not counted

R305.title: Ceiling Height
R305.summary: Minimum ceiling height requirements for habitable spaces.

subsections.R305.1: Minimum height - 7 ft for habitable spaces, hallways, bathrooms, toilet rooms, laundry rooms
subsections.R305.1.1: Basements - 6 ft 8 in. minimum under beams, girders, ducts (7 ft otherwise)
subsections.R305.2: Furred ceilings - Furred ceiling spaces not considered habitable

R309.title: Garages and Carports
R309.summary: Requirements for attached and detached garages and carports.

subsections.R309.1: Opening protection - Openings between garage and residence must be protected
subsections.R309.2: Separation required - 1/2 in. gypsum board or equivalent on garage side of walls and ceilings
subsections.R309.5: Fire sprinklers - Not required in garages when separated per R302.5 and R302.6

R311.title: Means of Egress
R311.summary: Exit requirements for residential buildings including doors, hallways, and stairways.

subsections.R311.2: Door type and size - 3 ft wide, 6 ft 8 in. high minimum
subsections.R311.3: Floors and landings - Required at exterior doors, landing length = door width or 36 in.
subsections.R311.7: Stairways - Min 36 in. width, max 7-3/4 in. riser, min 10 in. tread
subsections.R311.7.5: Handrails - Required on at least one side, 34-38 in. height

R313.title: Automatic Fire Sprinkler Systems
R313.summary: Requirements for fire sprinkler systems in residential dwellings.

subsections.R313.1: Townhouse automatic fire sprinkler systems - Required in townhouses per NFPA 13D or IRC P2904
subsections.R313.2: One- and two-family dwellings - Required per NFPA 13D or IRC P2904
subsections.R313.3: Exempt locations - Garages, open attached porches, detached structures, attics, crawl spaces

R322.title: Flood-Resistant Construction
R322.summary: Requirements for buildings located in flood hazard areas.

subsections.R322.1: General - Buildings in flood hazard areas shall comply with this section and ASCE 24
subsections.R322.1.2: Establishment of flood hazard areas - Per FEMA flood maps
subsections.R322.2: Flood hazard areas (non-coastal) - Lowest floor elevated to or above BFE
subsections.R322.3: Coastal high-hazard areas (V Zones) - Elevated on pilings or columns, bottom of lowest horizontal structural member at or above BFE

subsections.R401.1: Application - Foundation design per geotechnical conditions
subsections.R401.2: Requirements - Foundations shall be designed per accepted engineering practice or prescriptive requirements
subsections.R401.3: Drainage - Ground shall slope away from foundation walls min 6 in. in first 10 ft

R403.title: Footings
R403.summary: Foundation footing requirements including minimum dimensions and frost depth.

subsections.R403.1: General - Footings shall be supported on undisturbed natural soils or engineered fill
subsections.R403.1.1: Minimum size - 12 in. wide for 1-story, 15 in. for 2-story, 18 in. for 3-story (2,000 psf soil)
subsections.R403.1.4: Minimum depth - Below frost line per Table R301.2(1)
subsections.R403.1.6: Foundation anchorage - 1/2 in. anchor bolts at 6 ft OC, min 7 in. embedment

R404.title: Foundation and Retaining Walls
R404.summary: Requirements for foundation walls including concrete, masonry, and wood.

subsections.R404.1.1: Design of concrete foundation walls - Plain or reinforced per this section
subsections.R404.1.3: Design lateral support - Foundation walls laterally supported at top and bottom
subsections.R404.3: Wood sill plates - Treated, anchored per R403.1.6

R502.title: Wood Floor Framing
R502.summary: Requirements for wood floor joists, beams, and girders.

subsections.R502.3: Allowable joist spans - Per Tables R502.3.1(1) and R502.3.1(2)
subsections.R502.6: Bearing requirements - Min 1-1/2 in. on wood/metal, 3 in. on masonry/concrete

R602.title: Wood Wall Framing
R602.summary: Requirements for wood-framed walls including stud size, spacing, and bracing.

subsections.R602.3: Design and construction - Studs 2x4 at 16 in. OC or 2x6 at 24 in. OC
subsections.R602.3.1: Stud size, height, and spacing per Table R602.3(5)
subsections.R602.6: Drilling and notching - Max bored hole 40% of depth, max notch 25% of depth
subsections.R602.10: Wall bracing - Braced wall panels per Table R602.10.3(3)

R703.title: Exterior Covering
R703.summary: Requirements for exterior wall coverings and water-resistive barriers.

subsections.R703.1: General - Exterior walls shall provide weather protection
subsections.R703.1.1: Water-resistive barrier - Required behind exterior wall covering (WRB, housewrap)
subsections.R703.2: Flashing - Required at exterior wall openings, intersections, changes in material
subsections.R703.4: Wood, hardboard, and wood structural panel siding
subsections.R703.7: Stone and masonry veneer - Anchored per Section R703.7

R802.title: Wood Roof Framing
R802.summary: Requirements for rafters, ceiling joists, and roof trusses.

subsections.R802.4: Allowable rafter spans - Per Tables R802.4.1(1) through (8)
subsections.R802.11: Roof tie-down - Uplift resistance per Table R802.11

R806.title: Roof Ventilation
R806.summary: Requirements for ventilation of attic and roof spaces.

subsections.R806.1: Ventilation required - Enclosed attics and rafter spaces shall be ventilated
subsections.R806.2: Minimum vent area - 1/150 of ventilated space area (1/300 with balanced vents or vapor retarder)
subsections.R806.3: Vent and insulation clearance - Min 1 in. between insulation and roof sheathing
subsections.R806.4: Conditioned attic assemblies - Exception to ventilation with air-impermeable insulation
subsections.R806.5: Unvented attic and unvented enclosed rafter assemblies - Conditions for unvented attics

R905.title: Roofing Materials and Installation
R905.summary: Requirements for various roofing materials and their installation.

subsections.R905.1: Roofing application - Per manufacturer instructions and code requirements
subsections.R905.2: Asphalt shingles - ASTM D3462 or D7158, min slope 2:12, underlayment required
subsections.R905.3: Clay and concrete tile - ASTM C1167 or C1492, min slope 2.5:12
subsections.R905.5: Metal roof panels - ASTM E1592 or UL 580
subsections.R905.10: Built-up roofing - ASTM D226 or D4869
